channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Ashburn
item.description: May 5, 05:51 UTCUpdate - This incident affects a subset of Cloudflare Network Interconnects (CNI) 2.0 only.May 5, 05:44 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.May 5, 05:38 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with network performance in...

item.title: Azure - West US 2 (Washington): INC0156645
item.description: May 5, 05:10 UTCUpdate - Current status: We've identified an issue affecting a subset of data files, which is resulting in a delay for account usage updates. We're working on identifying a mitigation to restore normal processing. We'll provide another update within two hours.Customer experience:...

item.title: Physically Native World Models: A Hamiltonian Perspective on Generative World Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2605.00412v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: World models have recently re-emerged as a central paradigm for embodied intelligence, robotics, autonomous driving, and model-based reinforcement learning. However, current world model research is often dominated by three partially separated routes:...

item.title: AEM: Adaptive Entropy Modulation for Multi-Turn Agentic Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2605.00425v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) has significantly advanced the ability of large language model (LLM) agents to interact with environments and solve multi-turn tasks. Yet effective training remains challenging, as sparse, outcome-only rewards make it...

item.title: Thinking in Text and Images: Interleaved Vision--Language Reasoning Traces for Long-Horizon Robot Manipulation
item.description: arXiv:2605.00438v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-horizon robotic manipulation requires plans that are both logically coherent and geometrically grounded. Existing Vision-Language-Action policies usually hide planning in latent states or expose only one modality: text-only chain-of-thought...

item.title: On the Role of Artificial Intelligence in Human-Machine Symbiosis
item.description: arXiv:2605.00440v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The evolution of artificial intelligence (AI) has rendered the boundary between humanity and computational machinery increasingly ambiguous. In the presence of more interwoven relationships within human-machine symbiosis, the very notion of...

item.title: Instance-Aware Parameter Configuration in Bilevel Late Acceptance Hill Climbing for the Electric Capacitated Vehicle Routing Problem
item.description: arXiv:2605.00572v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Algorithm performance in combinatorial optimization is highly sensitive to parameter settings, while a single globally tuned configuration often fails to exploit the heterogeneity of instances. This limitation is particularly evident in the Electric...

item.title: Agentopic: A Generative AI Agent Workflow for Explainable Topic Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2605.00833v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentopic is a novel agent-based workflow for explainable topic modeling that leverages the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). Existing topic modeling approaches such as Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) and BERTopic often lack...

item.title: Polynomial-Time Optimal Group Selection via the Double-Commutator Eigenvalue Problem
item.description: arXiv:2605.00834v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The algebraic diversity framework replaces temporal averaging over multiple observations with algebraic group action on a single observation for second-order statistical estimation. The central open problem in this framework is $\textit{group...

item.title: Sparse Regression under Correlation and Weak Signals: A Reproducible Benchmark of Classical and Bayesian Methods
item.description: arXiv:2605.00835v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Choosing between classical and Bayesian sparse regression methods involves a real trade-off: penalized estimators like Lasso run in milliseconds but give no uncertainty estimates,while Horseshoe and Spike-and-Slab priors produce full posteriors but...

item.title: From Euler to Dormand-Prince: ODE Solvers for Flow Matching Generative Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.00836v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sampling from Flow Matching generative models requires solving an ordinary differential equation (ODE) whose computational cost is dominated by neural network forward passes. We derive four classical ODE solvers -- Euler, Explicit Midpoint, Classical...

item.title: Fast Log-Domain Sinkhorn Optimal Transport with Warp-Level GPU Reductions
item.description: arXiv:2605.00837v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Entropic regularized optimal transport (OT) via the Sinkhorn algorithm has become a fundamental tool in machine learning, yet existing implementations either suffer from numerical instability for small regularization parameters or incur significant...

item.title: H-Probes: Extracting Hierarchical Structures From Latent Representations of Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.00847v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Representing and navigating hierarchy is a fundamental primitive of reasoning. Large language models have demonstrated proficiency in a wide variety of tasks requiring hierarchical reasoning, but there exists limited analysis on how the models...

item.title: DIAGRAMS: A Review Framework for Reasoning-Level Attribution in Diagram QA
item.description: arXiv:2605.00905v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diagram question answering (Diagram QA) requires reasoning-level attribution that links each question-answer pair to all visual regions needed to derive the answer, rather than only the region containing the final response. Creating such structured...

item.title: Model Organisms Are Leaky: Perplexity Differencing Often Reveals Finetuning Objectives
item.description: arXiv:2605.00994v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Finetuning can significantly modify the behavior of large language models, including introducing harmful or unsafe behaviors. To study these risks, researchers develop model organisms: models finetuned to exhibit specific known behaviors for...

item.title: Can AI Debias the News? LLM Interventions Improve Cross-Partisan Receptivity but LLMs Overestimate Their Own Effectiveness
item.description: arXiv:2605.01006v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Partisan news media erode cross-partisan trust, but large language models (LLMs) offer a potential means of debiasing such content at scale. Across two pre-registered experiments, we tested whether LLM-generated debiasing of liberal news headlines...

item.title: CLEAR: Revealing How Noise and Ambiguity Degrade Reliability in LLMs for Medicine
item.description: arXiv:2605.01011v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Medical large language model (LLM) evaluations rely on simplified, exam-style benchmarks that rarely reflect the ambiguity of real-world medical inquiries. We introduce the CLinical Evaluation of Ambiguity and Reliability (CLEAR) framework, which...

item.title: Network hardware failure in Hong Kong
item.description: May 5, 00:57 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 5, 00:44 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.May 4, 22:00 UTCInvestigating - Reduced redundancy due to network hardware failure in Hong Kong

item.title: [AINews] The Other vs The Utility
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on the nature of AI "character" in the Clippy vs Anton debate

item.title: Increased build dequeue duration in Workers Builds
item.description: May 4, 20:20 UTCInvestigating - Some Workers Builds users may experience increased build dequeue times, or builds that are stuck in a queued state indefinitely.

item.title: Cisco Moves to Acquire Astrix Security to Tackle Non-Human Identity Risks
item.description: The acquisition strengthens Cisco’s push into identity-centric security for AI and machine access. The post Cisco Moves to Acquire Astrix Security to Tackle Non-Human Identity Risks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Phishing Campaign Hits 80+ Orgs Using SimpleHelp and ScreenConnect RMM Tools
item.description: An active phishing campaign has been observed targeting multiple vectors since at least April 2025, with legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software as a way to establish persistent remote access to compromised hosts. The activity, codenamed VENOMOUS#HELPER, has impacted over 80...

item.title: Trellix Source Code Repository Breached
item.description: The cybersecurity firm’s investigation has not found any impact on its source code release or distribution process. The post Trellix Source Code Repository Breached appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Issue affecting some pages on the ChatGPT website
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.

item.title: Incident with Issues and Webhooks
item.description: May 4, 16:40 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.May 4, 16:36 UTCMonitoring - The degradation has been mitigated. We are monitoring to ensure...

item.title: Progress Patches Critical MOVEit Automation Bug Enabling Authentication Bypass
item.description: Progress Software has released updates to address two security flaws in MOVEit Automation, including a critical bug that could result in an authentication bypass. MOVEit Automation (formerly Central) is a secure, server-based managed file transfer (MFT) solution used to schedule and automate file...

item.title: The distillation panic
item.description: ‘Distillation attacks’ is a horrible term for what is happening right now.

item.title: Week one of the Musk v. Altman trial: What it was like in the room
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Two of the most powerful people in AI—Sam Altman and Elon Musk—began their face-off in court in Oakland, California, last week. Musk is suing OpenAI,...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: May 4, 14:33 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 4, 14:07 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

item.title: Workers Analytics increased query failures
item.description: May 4, 14:29 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 4, 14:19 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare has identified an issue impacting a subset of Workers Analytics queries returning a 403 error. This does not impact writing Analytics Engine events.We are working to mitigate this problem. More...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: AI-Powered Phishing, Android Spying Tool, Linux Exploit, GitHub RCE & More
item.description: This week, the shadows moved faster than the patches. While most teams were still triaging last month’s alerts, attackers had already turned control panels into kill switches, kernels into open doors, and open-source pipelines into silent delivery systems. The game has shifted from breach to...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.5 and Sonnet 4.5
item.description: May 4, 13:59 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 33 Deals Announced in April 2026
item.description: Significant cybersecurity M&A deals announced by Airbus, Cyera, Fortra, Palo Alto Networks, Silverfort, and Socket. The post Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 33 Deals Announced in April 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Tailoring AI solutions for health care needs
item.description: The AI market is full of big promises of grand transformation. Health care is a prime target for those promises, beset as it is by financial pressures, labor shortages, and the growing burden of caring for an aging population. AI developers are targeting functions that vary widely, from curing...

item.title: DigiCert Revokes Certificates After Support Portal Hack
item.description: Hackers delivered malware via a customer chat channel, infected an analyst’s system, and accessed the internal support portal. The post DigiCert Revokes Certificates After Support Portal Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: 2026: The Year of AI-Assisted Attacks
item.description: On December 4, 2025, a 17-year-old was arrested in Osaka under Japan’s Unauthorized Access Prohibition Act. The young man had run malicious code to extract the personal data of over 7 million users of Kaikatsu Club, Japan's largest internet cafe chain. When asked, the young man shared his...

item.title: Silver Fox Deploys ABCDoor Malware via Tax-Themed Phishing in India and Russia
item.description: The China-based cybercrime group known as Silver Fox has been linked to a new campaign targeting organizations in Russia and India with a new malware called ABCDoor. The activity involved using phishing emails that mimic correspondence from the Income Tax Department of India in December 2025,...

item.title: Exploitation of ‘Copy Fail’ Linux Vulnerability Begins
item.description: CISA has added the bug to its KEV list, and Microsoft has observed limited exploitation, mainly associated with PoC testing. The post Exploitation of ‘Copy Fail’ Linux Vulnerability Begins appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Turnstile Challenge Issues
item.description: May 4, 09:59 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.May 4, 09:04 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating a potential issue with the Turnstile challenge platform. Users may experience failed challenges solve attempts.Further details will be provided as...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.5
item.description: May 4, 09:49 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 4, 09:23 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 4, 09:14 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue.May 4, 08:19 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is...

item.title: [AINews] AI Engineer World's Fair — Autoresearch, Memory, World Models, Tokenmaxxing, Agentic Commerce, and Vertical AI Call for Speakers
item.description: a quiet day lets us make a call for speakers!

item.title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
item.description: In the first week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk took the stand in a crisp black suit and tie and argued that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman had deceived him into bankrolling the company. Along the way, he warned that AI could destroy us all and sat...

item.title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
item.description: Cybersecurity was already under strain before AI entered the stack. Now, as AI expands the attack surface and adds new complexity, the limits of legacy approaches are becoming harder to ignore. This session from MIT Technology Review’s EmTech AI conference explores why security must be...

item.title: Elevated error rate for Responses API
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Responses (Operational)

item.title: Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
item.description: Companies are taking control of their own data to tailor AI for their needs. The challenge lies in balancing ownership with the safe, trusted flow of high‑quality data needed to power reliable insights. This conversation from MIT Technology Review’s EmTech AI conference examines how AI...

item.title: Elevated error rates for image generation
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Images (Operational) Image Generation (Operational)

item.title: Preparing for a ‘vulnerability patch wave’
item.description: Organisations must act now to prepare for a wave of patches that will address decades of technical debt.

item.title: [AINews] Agents for Everything Else: Codex for Knowledge Work, Claude for Creative Work
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on coding agents "breaking containment"

item.title: Elevated error rates affecting ChatGPT for some users in Europe
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.
